pattern: A bulb's final state only depends on how many times it was toggled, so a fixed-size on/off tracker indexed by bulb number replaces simulating a giant grid, and parity of the toggle count is all that matters.
hints: You only ever need 100 slots -- one boolean per bulb number from 1 to 100 -- regardless of how long the input array is.
Flip the boolean at index bulbs[i]-1 for every value in the input array, in order; the final boolean tells you the bulb's end state.
After processing every value, scan bulb numbers 1 through 100 in order and collect the ones left on -- the scan order already gives you the sorted result for free.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, one bulb toggled twice cancels out
statement example, same bulb toggled twice ends off
a bulb toggled an odd number of times (three) ends on, not off
every bulb toggled exactly once -- all end on, exercising the full sorted range
no repeats at all, already ascending

stress.description: random toggle sequences across the full bulb range and length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    bulbs = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [bulbs]
